### Define TemplateError in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v2.1.4/errors/page.mdx

The TemplateError is thrown when a template is incompatible with the current SDK version, typically due to using an older envd version. It includes a message and an optional stack trace, guiding users on necessary template updates.

```typescript
class TemplateError {
  constructor(message: string, stackTrace?: string)
}
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize New Sandbox Template with SDK

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v2.2.4/template/page.mdx

Initializes a new sandbox template using the E2B SDK. Allows specifying the path, template name, and target language (typescript, python-sync, python-async).

```bash
e2b template init-v2 [options]
```

--------------------------------

### Define TemplateError in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v2.1.5/errors/page.mdx

Defines the TemplateError class, thrown when a template is incompatible with the current SDK version. It takes a message and an optional stack trace.

```TypeScript
class TemplateError extends Error {
  constructor(message: string, stackTrace?: string) {
    super(message);
    this.name = "TemplateError";
    if (stackTrace) {
      this.stack = stackTrace;
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Define TemplateError in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.5.0/errors/page.mdx

Thrown when a template uses an outdated version of envd, indicating incompatibility with the current SDK. This error inherits from SandboxError.

```typescript
class TemplateError extends SandboxError {
  message: string;

  constructor(message: string) {
    super(message);
    this.message = message;
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Define TemplateError in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.3.0/errors/page.mdx

Defines the TemplateError class, thrown when a template is incompatible with the current SDK version due to outdated envd usage. It requires a string message.

```typescript
class TemplateError {
  constructor(message: string): TemplateError
}
```

--------------------------------

### Define TemplateError in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.7/errors/page.mdx

Thrown when a template uses an incompatible, older version of the envd environment. This error indicates a version mismatch issue in the E2B template system. It expects a string message.

```typescript
class TemplateError extends Error {
  constructor(message: string) {
    super(message);
    this.name = "TemplateError";
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Sandbox Creation API

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.1/sandbox/page.mdx

Provides functionality to create new sandboxes from specified templates with various configuration options.

```APIDOC
## POST /sandboxes

### Description
Creates a new sandbox instance from a specified sandbox template.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/sandboxes

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **template** (string) - Required - Sandbox template name or ID.
- **opts** (SandboxOpts) - Optional - Connection and sandbox options.

### Request Body
```json
{
  "template": "<template-name-or-id>",
  "opts": {
    "accessToken": "string",
    "apiKey": "string",
    "debug": "boolean",
    "domain": "string",
    "envs": {
      "key": "value"
    },
    "logger": "Logger",
    "metadata": {
      "key": "value"
    },
    "requestTimeoutMs": "number",
    "timeoutMs": "number"
  }
}
```

### Request Example
```ts
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create('<template-name-or-id>', {
  timeoutMs: 60000,
  metadata: { projectId: 'my-project' }
});
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **sandbox** (Sandbox) - The created sandbox instance.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "sandbox": "// Sandbox instance object"
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Create a New Sandbox Instance (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.0.0/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Creates a new E2B cloud sandbox instance. Users can specify a template, timeout, metadata, environment variables, and API key for authentication. Defaults to the 'base' template if none is provided. Use this class method instead of the constructor.

```python
class AsyncSandbox(SandboxSetup, SandboxApi)

@classmethod
async def create(cls,
                 template: Optional[str] = None,
                 timeout: Optional[int] = None,
                 metadata: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
                 envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
                 api_key: Optional[str] = None,
                 domain: Optional[str] = None,
                 debug: Optional[bool] = None,
                request_timeout: Optional[float] = None)
```

--------------------------------

### TypeScript: TemplateError Constructor

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.5/errors/page.mdx

Indicates an error when a template uses an incompatible, older version of envd. It requires a string message.

```typescript
new TemplateError(message: string): TemplateError
```

--------------------------------

### Define TemplateException in Python

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.0.3/exceptions/page.mdx

Specifically raised when a sandbox template uses an outdated version of the environment that is incompatible with the current SDK. This helps in managing template versioning issues.

```python
class TemplateException(SandboxException)

```

--------------------------------

### Define TemplateException for Sandbox Compatibility in Python

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.0.1/exceptions/page.mdx

Defines the 'TemplateException' inheriting from 'SandboxException'. This exception is raised when a sandbox template uses an outdated version of envd that is incompatible with the current SDK, signaling a need for template updates.

```python
class TemplateException(SandboxException):
    pass
```

--------------------------------

### Create Sandbox

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.5.5/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Creates a new sandbox environment. You can specify a template, timeout, metadata, environment variables, and other configuration options.

```APIDOC
## POST /sandboxes

### Description
Create a new sandbox.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/sandboxes

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **template** (str) - Optional - Sandbox template name or ID
- **timeout** (int) - Optional - Timeout for the sandbox in seconds, default to 300 seconds. Maximum time a sandbox can be kept alive is 24 hours (86_400 seconds) for Pro users and 1 hour (3_600 seconds) for Hobby users
- **metadata** (Dict[str, str]) - Optional - Custom metadata for the sandbox
- **envs** (Dict[str, str]) - Optional - Custom environment variables for the sandbox
- **secure** (bool) - Optional - Whether to create a secure sandbox
- **api_key** (str) - Optional - E2B API Key to use for authentication, defaults to `E2B_API_KEY` environment variable
- **domain** (str) - Optional - Domain to use for the request
- **debug** (bool) - Optional - Enable debug mode
- **sandbox_id** (str) - Optional - ID of an existing sandbox to use
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds
- **proxy** (ProxyTypes) - Optional - Proxy to use for the request and for the requests made to the returned sandbox

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **Sandbox** - sandbox instance for the new sandbox
```

--------------------------------

### Define TemplateError in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.1.0/errors/page.mdx

Defines the TemplateError class, thrown when a template uses an incompatible old envd version. It requires a string message for instantiation.

```ts
class TemplateError {
  constructor(message: string)
}
```

### Create Sandbox (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.5.4/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Creates a new sandbox instance. Accepts parameters for template, timeouts, metadata, environment variables, API key, domain, debug mode, request timeout, proxy, and security settings. Defaults to the 'base' template if none is specified. The maximum sandbox lifetime is limited based on user subscription.

```python
await AsyncSandbox.create(
    template: Optional[str] = None,
    timeout: Optional[int] = None,
    metadata: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
    envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
    api_key: Optional[str] = None,
    domain: Optional[str] = None,
    debug: Optional[bool] = None,
    request_timeout: Optional[float] = None,
    proxy: Optional[ProxyTypes] = None,
    secure: Optional[bool] = None
)
```

--------------------------------

```python
@classmethod

    # ... implementation details ...
    pass
```

Defines the TemplateError class, thrown when a template uses an incompatible old envd version. The constructor accepts a string message.

### Create Sandbox using Python

Creates a new sandbox instance with various configuration options. Supports specifying a template, timeout, auto-pause behavior, metadata, environment variables, security settings, and internet access. Defaults to using the base sandbox template.

```python
from typing import Optional, Dict
from unpack import Unpack

class ApiParams:
    pass

class Self:
    pass

class Sandbox:
    @classmethod
    async def beta_create(
        cls,
        template: Optional[str] = None,
        timeout: Optional[int] = None,
        auto_pause: bool = False,
        metadata: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
        envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
        secure: bool = True,
        allow_internet_access: bool = True,
        **opts: Unpack[ApiParams]
    ) -> Self:
        # Implementation details omitted for brevity
        pass
```

### Define TemplateError in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.12.0/errors/page.mdx

Represents an error when a template uses an incompatible or old version of the E2B environment. This error extends SandboxError and requires a string message.

```typescript
class TemplateError extends SandboxError {
  constructor(message: string) {
    super(message);
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize a new sandbox with default or custom parameters

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.3.1/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Creates a new sandbox instance. You can specify a custom template, timeout duration, metadata, environment variables, API key, domain, debug mode, sandbox ID, and request timeout. Defaults to the 'base' template if none is provided.

```python
from e2b import Sandbox

sandbox = Sandbox(template='python-3.11', timeout=600, metadata={'user_id': '123'}, envs={'MY_VAR': 'value'})
print(f'Sandbox created with ID: {sandbox.sandbox_id}')
```

--------------------------------

### Create Sandbox

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.11.0/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Creates a new sandbox instance. You can specify a template, timeout, environment variables, and other configurations for the sandbox.

```APIDOC
## POST /sandboxes

### Description
Creates a new sandbox. By default, the sandbox is created from the default `base` sandbox template.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/sandboxes

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **template** (string) - Optional - Sandbox template name or ID
- **timeout** (integer) - Optional - Timeout for the sandbox in seconds, default to 300 seconds. Maximum time a sandbox can be kept alive is 24 hours (86,400 seconds) for Pro users and 1 hour (3,600 seconds) for Hobby users.
- **metadata** (object) - Optional - Custom metadata for the sandbox
- **envs** (object) - Optional - Custom environment variables for the sandbox
- **api_key** (string) - Optional - E2B API Key to use for authentication, defaults to `E2B_API_KEY` environment variable
- **domain** (string) - Optional - The domain to use for the sandbox
- **debug** (boolean) - Optional - Enable debug mode for the sandbox
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds
- **proxy** (object) - Optional - Proxy to use for the request and for the requests made to the returned sandbox
- **secure** (boolean) - Optional - Secure the sandbox with an access token
- **allow_internet_access** (boolean) - Optional - Allow sandbox to access the internet, defaults to `True`

### Request Example
```json
{
  "template": "node",
  "timeout": 600,
  "envs": {
    "MY_VAR": "my_value"
  }
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (201)
- **sandbox_id** (string) - The ID of the newly created sandbox
- **template** (string) - The template used for the sandbox
- **status** (string) - The status of the sandbox

### Response Example
```json
{
  "sandbox_id": "sbx_abcdef123456",
  "template": "node",
  "status": "running"
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Create New Sandbox Instance (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.1.0/sandbox_async/page.mdx

The `create` class method is the recommended way to instantiate and initialize a new sandbox. It allows configuration of various parameters such as template, timeout, metadata, environment variables, API key, domain, debug mode, and request timeout. The default sandbox template is 'base'.

```python
@classmethod
async def create(cls,
                 template: Optional[str] = None,
                 timeout: Optional[int] = None,
                 metadata: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
                 envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
                 api_key: Optional[str] = None,
                 domain: Optional[str] = None,
                 debug: Optional[bool] = None,
                 request_timeout: Optional[float] = None)

```

--------------------------------

### Create Sandbox

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.8.0/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Creates a new sandbox instance. You can specify various configurations such as template, timeouts, environment variables, and metadata.

```APIDOC
## POST /e2b-api/sandboxes

### Description
Create a new sandbox. By default, the sandbox is created from the default `base` sandbox template.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/e2b-api/sandboxes

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **template** (string) - Optional - Sandbox template name or ID.
- **timeout** (integer) - Optional - Timeout for the sandbox in seconds, default to 300 seconds. Maximum time a sandbox can be kept alive is 24 hours (86_400 seconds) for Pro users and 1 hour (3_600 seconds) for Hobby users.
- **api_key** (string) - Optional - E2B API Key to use for authentication, defaults to `E2B_API_KEY` environment variable.
- **domain** (string) - Optional - The domain to use for the sandbox.
- **debug** (boolean) - Optional - Enable debug mode for the sandbox.
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds.
- **proxy** (object) - Optional - Proxy to use for the request and for the requests made to the returned sandbox.
- **secure** (boolean) - Optional - If true, the sandbox is secured with an access token and cannot be used without it.

#### Request Body
- **metadata** (object) - Optional - Custom metadata for the sandbox.
- **envs** (object) - Optional - Custom environment variables for the sandbox.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **sandbox_id** (string) - The ID of the newly created sandbox.
- **...other sandbox details**

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "sandbox_id": "sandbox-xxxxxxxxxxxx",
  "template": "base",
  "created_at": "2023-10-27T10:00:00Z",
  "host": "sandbox.e2b.dev",
  "instance_type": "default"
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Publish Sandbox Template with E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.7.0/template/page.mdx

Publishes a sandbox template, making it available for use. Options include specifying the path, configuration file, team ID, and flags to select templates interactively or skip confirmation.

```bash
e2b template publish [options] [template]

Options:
 - -p, --path <path>: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory 
 - --config <e2b-toml>: specify path to the E2B config toml. By default E2B tries to find ./e2b.toml in root directory. 
 - -s, --select: select sandbox template from interactive list 
 - -t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team). 
 - -y, --yes: skip manual publish confirmation
```

--------------------------------

### Publish Sandbox Template with E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.8.0/template/page.mdx

Publishes a sandbox template, making it available for use by others. Options include specifying the path, config file, team ID, and interactively selecting a template or skipping confirmation.

```bash
e2b template publish [options] [template]

Options:
  -p, --path <path>: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory
  --config <e2b-toml>: specify path to the E2B config toml. By default E2B tries to find ./e2b.toml in root directory.
  -s, --select: select sandbox template from interactive list
  -t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the team settings in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team).
  -y, --yes: skip manual publish confirmation
```
